dimensions.summary: Issue #130 defines the single semantic owner, entry evidence, closed loop, four exits, artifact reuse, script boundary, implementation scope, exclusions, and acceptance criteria without an unresolved product question.

The canonical guru-check-task package, Phase 2 route, existing artifact ownership migration, runtime support, additive distribution, and validation form one independently testable delivery unit; Branch Review, final overlay convergence, subtraction capability, and release remain downstream.

Current repository evidence identifies the canonical workflow, shared runtime, phase2-check schema/commands/tests, upstream-ownership spec, preset distribution, and platform package copies that planning must reconcile.

Live current-state review confirms that deterministic phase2 record/check support exists while the complete semantic check is still expanded across workflow behavior rather than owned by a guru-check-task closed loop.

The repository does not yet provide guru-check-task as the sole Phase 2 semantic owner with the required package contract, scope-before-severity gate, four typed exits, full-rerun fixtures, and additive distribution.

The durable upstream-ownership spec already points to guru-check-task, while runtime and tests preserve reusable deterministic foundations; #130 explicitly requires the coordinated workflow, package, schema/owner, test, dogfood, throwaway, and update/reapply change needed to align them.

Selected archived evidence consistently requires one phase2-check.json artifact, deterministic recorder/validator boundaries, complete dirty-snapshot freshness, and Docs SSOT reconciliation during Phase 2 rather than post-commit repair.

The reviewed adjacent issues are dependencies or downstream extensions, not substitutes: #131 owns post-commit Branch Review, #132 final integration, #108 subtraction capability, and #81 release; #130 remains the correct target.
The live open issue authority, updated timestamp, title/body identity, and retained-target clarification disposition were reread and remain current.
Context, clarity, and wording payloads are current, checker-compatible, content-consistent, and joined to the same issue/base/current/history/duplicate identities by the rebuilt linkage digest.

scope_conclusion.requirement_scope_basis: Live issue #130 plus current context, clear requirements, and passed controlled-wording evidence define the complete implementation boundary.
scope_conclusion.duplicate_reuse_decision: Retain #130; no reviewed open issue replaces its Phase 2 closed-loop Skill delivery.
scope_conclusion.implementation_target: Canonical guru-check-task package/interface/references, Phase 2 workflow route, existing phase2-check recorder/validator/schema ownership, additive platform/preset distribution, and validation fixtures.
scope_conclusion.current_gap: Phase 2 semantic ownership remains spread across workflow behavior and legacy Guru integration instead of one guru-check-task closed loop and one phase2-check.json owner.
archived_constraints: Reuse phase2-check.json; do not create a second Phase 2 pass artifact.
Bind the full dirty snapshot and preserve post-commit freshness semantics.
Complete Docs SSOT reconciliation during Phase 2; post-commit review only verifies it.
Keep semantic adequacy, scope qualification, severity, routing, and pass decisions in the AI Skill; scripts only execute, collect, record, and validate deterministic facts.
risk_boundary: Cover honest-but-fallible normal workflow paths, ordinary stale/mismatch/omission cases, implementation defects, and stated compatibility boundaries.
Qualify scope before severity; unplanned unusual security, threat, concurrency, fault-injection, locking, TOCTOU, and cross-OS hardening scenarios remain proposals or out of scope.
excluded_scope: Final upstream overlay convergence owned by #132.
Concrete code/docs subtraction capability owned by #108.
Any modification or overlay of upstream-owned trellis-check Skill, Agent, .trellis/agents/check.md, or other upstream check entry.
A universal static-analysis framework for every language or repository type.

ai_review_gate.summary: All ten readiness dimensions were semantically reviewed against the live #130 authority, current repository evidence, archived Phase 2 constraints, duplicate boundaries, clarification disposition, and wording classification; the independently deliverable unit is complete enough for workspace creation.

human_confirmation.reason: Live issue #130 already fixes the product semantics, delivery boundary, upstream ownership, artifact ownership, typed exits, and normal-operation risk boundary; this review introduces no new product decision.

reason: Issue #130 is one current, independently deliverable change request with complete linked prerequisites and no blocking readiness finding.

affected_evidence.summary: The current portable linkage across target, base, repository state, history, duplicates, clarification, and wording evidence.
